description_long: This object is a ground-mounted solar panel installation, featuring a rectangular array of six photovoltaic panels arranged in two rows of three. The panels are supported by a single vertical metal pole anchored into a concrete base, which is set on a patch of grass. The solar panels are encased in a metallic frame and are tilted at an angle to optimize sunlight capture. The mounting structure appears robust, likely made of galvanized steel, and the concrete base provides stability against wind and environmental forces. No visible electrical cabling or inverter components are present, and the installation is designed for outdoor use in urban or park environments.
description: A ground-mounted, pole-supported solar panel array with six panels, set on a concrete base over grass. The panels are tilted and framed in metal for outdoor solar energy collection.
description_view_0: Front view showing the underside of the tilted solar panel array, the supporting pole, and the concrete base.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the tilt angle of the solar panels and the thickness of the mounting structure.
description_view_2: Direct view of the solar panel faces, clearly showing the 2x3 grid arrangement and the supporting pole.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again emphasizing the tilt and the structural support of the pole and base.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 30 degrees, facing forward (relative to the pole), orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: pole-mounted, ground installation with a steel post and concrete base
attribute_array_configuration: 2x3 grid of solar panels in a single rectangular array
